__data__.text:
ZOIKOLOGIA™

ASK KRITON™ QUERY ORCHESTRATION

Canonical MVP Workflow — Engineering Specification

1. Executive Assessment

The submitted engineer workflow — query, retrieve, classify, compose, audit and respond — is a valid MVP skeleton. It is not sufficient as a production-facing specification for a source-governed, multi-tenant accounting intelligence platform.

This specification closes the material gaps that would otherwise create engineering ambiguity, compliance exposure, audit weakness or unsafe model behaviour.

2. Canonical Operating Principles

3. Canonical Flow

User submits query via Ask Kriton™
  |
  |  POST /api/v1/orchestration/ask  (Idempotency-Key required)
  v
orchestration/service.py
  |
  +-- 1. Generate identifiers
  |      query_id, correlation_id, request_id, audit_chain_id
  |      tenant_id and user_id/session_id resolved from auth context
  |
  +-- AUDIT: query_received
  |
  +-- 2. Validate request
  |      schema, tenant entitlement, permissions, rate limit, idempotency
  |      FAIL -> route = REJECTED -> AUDIT: request_rejected -> return
  |
  +-- AUDIT: request_validated
  |
  +-- 3. Pre-screen safety before retrieval
  |      prompt injection, malicious instruction, data exfiltration, unsupported request
  |      FAIL -> route = SECURITY_INCIDENT or REFUSAL -> persist incident/refusal -> return
  |
  +-- AUDIT: pre_safety_screen_completed
  |
  +-- 4.

Zl Eng 02 Ask Kriton Query Orchestration Canonical Mvp Workflow
Zl Eng 02 Ask Kriton Query Orchestration Canonical Mvp Workflow

__data__.text:
Retrieve SourceBundle [Massarius™ retrieval layer]
  |      orchestration/retrieve.py
  |      MVP: category-keyword source selection; pre-RAG; do not label as RAG
  |      tenant-scoped source_library query with tenant_id enforced at data layer
  |      returns SourceBundle, confidence_state, exclusion_reasons
  |      FAIL -> route = CLARIFICATION or REFUSAL
  |
  +-- AUDIT: retrieval_started + retrieval_completed / retrieval_failed
  |
  +-- 5. Classify professional risk [risk_safety]
  |      LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / RESTRICTED
  |      route selected from versioned policy matrix
  |      classifier_version and policy_version recorded
  |
  +-- AUDIT: risk_classified + route_selected
  |
  +-- 6. Execute deterministic route
  |      LLM -> model_gateway (MVP: MockProviderAdapter.complete())
  |      REFUSAL -> safe refusal response
  |      HUMAN_REVIEW -> persisted review_case object
  |      CLARIFICATION -> bounded clarifying question
  |      SECURITY_INCIDENT -> persisted incident object and minimal safe response
  |
  +-- AUDIT: route_execution_completed / composition_failed
  |
  +-- 7. Post-composition validation (LLM route only)
  |      grounding check, citation binding, prohibited-claim scan
  |      FAIL -> composition_rejected -> route degrades to REFUSAL or HUMAN_REVIEW
  |
  +-- 8. Post-process response
  |      citations, confidence_state, safety state, disclaimers, redaction of internals
  |
  +-- AUDIT: response_finalised + response_returned
  |
  v
Structured response contract returned to frontend

4.

__data__.text:
Endpoint and Request Envelope

The orchestration endpoint receives a user query submitted through Ask Kriton™ and returns a route-driven response contract. The endpoint must be idempotent, tenant-aware and audit-bound.

POST /api/v1/orchestration/ask
Required header: Idempotency-Key: <client-generated-key>

5. Identifier Model

6. Validation and Pre-Screen Safety

Request validation and pre-screen safety run before source retrieval. This ordering is a release gate.

7. Retrieval Layer: Massarius™ Source Retrieval MVP

The current retrieval layer is a category-keyword source-selection stand-in. It is pre-RAG. It validates source eligibility, safety routing and audit flow before semantic retrieval is introduced.

The term “RAG” must not be used in code comments, documentation or external materials until embeddings, chunking, semantic retrieval, ranking, re-ranking, citation binding, retrieval evaluation, hallucination checks and source freshness handling are implemented.

7.1 Tenant Isolation

Every source_library query must carry tenant_id at the data-access layer. Application-level filtering alone is not sufficient. Row-level security or an equivalent lower-level enforcement mechanism is required where available.

7.2 SourceBundle Schema

{
  "source_bundle_id": "sb_...",
  "eligible_sources": [],
  "excluded_sources": [],
  "exclusion_reasons": [],
  "jurisdiction": "UK | US | IFRS | ...",
  "source_types": ["standard", "guidance", "internal_policy"],
  "authority_level": "primary | secondary | internal",
  "freshness_state": "current | stale | unknown",
  "licence_state": "permitted | restricted | unknown",
  "retrieval_method": "keyword_mvp",
  "confidence_state": "sufficient | limited | insufficient | conflicting_sources | stale_sources | restricted_sources"
}

8.

Zl Eng 02 Ask Kriton Query Orchestration Canonical Mvp Workflow
Zl Eng 02 Ask Kriton Query Orchestration Canonical Mvp Workflow

__data__.text:
Deterministic Risk Classification and Routing

Routes are selected only from the versioned policy matrix below. The matrix is the single source of truth. Each route_selected audit event records classifier_version and policy_version, making the decision reproducible.

8.1 Route Rules

The model gateway executes only when route == LLM.

Clarification is bounded to two cycles per query_id. The third unresolved cycle escalates to HUMAN_REVIEW.

HUMAN_REVIEW creates a persisted review_case object; returning an escalation label without a persisted object is non-compliant.

SECURITY_INCIDENT creates a persisted incident object with the triggering classification, correlation_id and evidence reference.

REFUSAL returns a safe user-facing message and records refusal_returned in the audit chain.

9. Model Gateway and Composition Controls

The model gateway sits behind a provider abstraction. The MVP implementation may use MockProviderAdapter.complete(), but the interface must be production-shaped from the start.

10. Post-Composition Validation

Route gating before composition is not sufficient. LLM output must pass an output-side gate before response finalisation.

11.

__data__.text:
10. Post-Composition Validation

Route gating before composition is not sufficient. LLM output must pass an output-side gate before response finalisation.

11. Persisted Objects for Escalation and Incident Handling

11.1 Human Review Object

{
  "review_case_id": "hr_123",
  "query_id": "qry_123",
  "tenant_id": "ten_123",
  "risk_level": "HIGH",
  "confidence_state": "conflicting_sources",
  "reason": "Professional judgement or source conflict requires review",
  "assigned_queue": "accounting_review",
  "status": "open",
  "created_at": "ISO 8601 UTC"
}

11.2 Security Incident Object

{
  "incident_id": "sec_123",
  "query_id": "qry_123",
  "correlation_id": "corr_123",
  "tenant_id": "ten_123",
  "trigger": "prompt_injection | data_exfiltration | malicious_instruction",
  "evidence_reference": "encrypted_ref_123",
  "status": "open",
  "created_at": "ISO 8601 UTC"
}

12. Frontend Response Contract

The frontend renders from route and outcome. It must not infer state by parsing answer text. One contract shape is used for all routes; unused fields are null.

},
  "audit_reference": { "audit_chain_id": "aud_123" }
}

13. Audit Ledger

The audit ledger is a trust-control system, not operational logging. It must provide durable, ordered, tamper-evident evidence for every query lifecycle.

13.1 Required Audit Events

query_received
request_validated
request_rejected
pre_safety_screen_completed
retrieval_started
retrieval_completed
retrieval_failed
risk_classified
route_selected
composition_started
composition_completed
composition_failed
composition_rejected
human_review_created
refusal_returned
clarification_returned
security_incident_recorded
response_finalised
response_returned

13.2 Event Schema

{
  "event_id": "evt_123",
  "audit_chain_id": "aud_123",
  "correlation_id": "corr_123",
  "query_id": "qry_123",
  "tenant_id": "ten_123",
  "step": "risk_classified",
  "actor": "system",
  "timestamp": "ISO 8601 UTC",
  "classifier_version": "rc_1.0",
  "policy_version": "pm_1.0",
  "input_hash": "...",
  "output_hash": "...",
  "previous_event_hash": "...",
  "event_hash": "...",
  "metadata": {}
}

14. Observability, Testing and Release Gates

14.1 Observability

14.2 Minimum Test Coverage

Pre-screen failure short-circuits retrieval and model execution.

Every policy matrix row resolves to the expected route.

Tenant A cannot retrieve Tenant B sources under any query path.

A model output with unsupported claims triggers composition_rejected.

A citation not present in eligible_sources triggers composition_rejected.

Clarification escalates after two unresolved cycles.

Audit write failure prevents answer return.

Duplicate idempotency key returns the original terminal result, not a second execution.

Frontend renders route/outcome states without parsing answer text.

Clarification escalates after two unresolved cycles.

Audit write failure prevents answer return.

Duplicate idempotency key returns the original terminal result, not a second execution.

Frontend renders route/outcome states without parsing answer text.

14.3 Release Gates

15. Approval Conditions

Ask Kriton™ remains the user-facing entry point; orchestration/service.py remains the orchestration controller.

retrieve.py is labelled as MVP keyword retrieval; the term RAG is prohibited until Section 7 criteria are met.

Request validation and pre-screen safety execute before retrieval, with terminal failure routes.

Tenant-scoped retrieval is enforced at the data-access layer with a release-gating isolation test.

SourceBundle is implemented per Section 7.2 and confidence_state drives routing.

Routing is implemented from the versioned policy matrix in Section 8; classifier_version and policy_version are recorded for each decision.

Model gateway executes only on route == LLM and includes timeout, retry, circuit breaker and refusal-safe failure.

Post-composition validation is implemented with composition_rejected path.

HUMAN_REVIEW and SECURITY_INCIDENT create persisted objects.

Audit ledger meets Section 13 requirements.

Frontend consumes the Section 12 contract and renders from route/outcome only.

16. Final CTO Verdict

Approved as the canonical MVP specification subject to the release gates and approval conditions above. The engineer’s submitted workflow is retained as the baseline orchestration spine, but the corrected specification of record is this document.

No production or customer-facing release should proceed unless deterministic routing, tenant-isolated retrieval, post-composition validation and durable audit integrity are demonstrably implemented and tested.

17. Decision Record

18. Sign-Off
